# ============================================================================
# WORKER AGENT CLASS
# ============================================================================


class WorkerAgent:
    """
    Homogeneous recursive worker using genuine ReAct loop.

    The LLM sees the full conversation history on every iteration
    and decides which tool to call next based on observations.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        llm_adapter: Any,  # Has async .chat(messages) method
        mcp_client: Any,  # Has async .call_tool(name, args) method
        blackboard: Blackboard,
        event_callback: Optional[
            Any
        ] = None,  # async (event_type: str, data: dict) -> None
    ):
        """
        Initialize worker agent.

        Args:
            llm_adapter: LLM adapter with chat() method
            mcp_client: MCP client with call_tool() method
            blackboard: Shared context store
            event_callback: Optional async callback for emitting SSE events (e.g. tool_update)
        """
        self.llm = llm_adapter
        self.mcp = mcp_client
        self.blackboard = blackboard
        self._event_callback = event_callback

        # Worker numbering state — shared across all invocations of this agent
        self._top_level_counter: int = 0
        self._child_counters: Dict[str, int] = {}  # parent_number → next child index
        self._number_registry: Dict[str, str] = {}  # worker_id → worker_number

    # ========================================================================
    # WORKER NUMBERING
    # ========================================================================

    def _assign_number(self, worker_id: str, parent_worker_id: Optional[str]) -> str:
        """
        Assign a hierarchical log number to a worker.

        Top-level workers (no parent) get sequential integers: "1", "2", "3" …
        Child workers get dot-notation: "1.1", "1.2", "2.1" …

        The mapping is stored in _number_registry so children can look up
        their parent's number when spawn_worker spawns a grandchild.
        """
        if parent_worker_id is None or parent_worker_id not in self._number_registry:
            # Top-level worker
            self._top_level_counter += 1
            num = str(self._top_level_counter)
        else:
            parent_num = self._number_registry[parent_worker_id]
            self._child_counters[parent_num] = (
                self._child_counters.get(parent_num, 0) + 1
            )
            num = f"{parent_num}.{self._child_counters[parent_num]}"

        self._number_registry[worker_id] = num
        return num

    # ========================================================================
    # ENTRY POINT
    # ========================================================================

    async def execute(
        self,
        project_id: str,
        task_description: str,
        entity_type: str,
        temp_id: str,
        is_first_tier: bool = True,
        parent_worker_id: Optional[str] = None,
        dependency_temp_ids: Optional[List[str]] = None,
        checkpoint_rules: Optional[List[CheckpointRule]] = None,
        recursion_depth: int = 0,
        target_artifact_id: Optional[str] = None,
        assigned_classes: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
        parent_context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        planned_artifact_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> SubAgentResult:
        """
        Execute worker task and return structured result.

        Args:
            project_id: Project identifier
            task_description: What to create or update (human-readable)
            entity_type: Entity type to create or update (single type)
            temp_id: Temporary identifier for planning
            is_first_tier: Whether this is a first-tier worker
            parent_worker_id: ID of parent worker if spawned
            dependency_temp_ids: Temp IDs this worker depends on
            checkpoint_rules: HITL checkpoint rules
            recursion_depth: Current depth in worker hierarchy (0 = root)
            target_artifact_id: If provided, update this existing artifact instead of creating new
            assigned_classes: (SOTA) Subsystem-scoped metamodel class restrictions
            parent_context: (SOTA) Interface contracts from parent subsystems (temp_ids, intents)

        Returns:
            SubAgentResult with success status and artifacts
        """
        worker_id = f"worker_{uuid4().hex[:8]}"
        worker_number = self._assign_number(worker_id, parent_worker_id)
        start = datetime.utcnow()

        state = WorkerState(
            worker_id=worker_id,
            project_id=project_id,
            entity_type=entity_type,
            temp_id=temp_id,
            task_description=task_description,
            is_first_tier=is_first_tier,
            parent_worker_id=parent_worker_id,
            checkpoint_rules=checkpoint_rules or [],
            worker_number=worker_number,
            recursion_depth=recursion_depth,
            target_artifact_id=target_artifact_id,
            created_artifact_id=target_artifact_id,  # Start with target if updating
            planned_artifact_id=planned_artifact_id,
            assigned_classes=assigned_classes
            or [],  # SOTA: Subsystem class restrictions
            parent_context=parent_context
            or {},  # SOTA: Interface contracts from parent subsystems
        )

        # Set agent context for logging
        set_agent_context("worker", worker_number)

        tier_label = "first-tier" if is_first_tier else f"child of {parent_worker_id}"
        logger.info(
            f"[worker {worker_number}] Starting: {entity_type} "
            f"(temp_id={temp_id}, {tier_label})"
        )

        try:
            await self._initialize(state, dependency_temp_ids or [])
            await self._react_loop(state)
        except SubAgentAbortedError as e:
            logger.warning(f"[worker {worker_number}] Aborted: {e.failure_reason}")
            clear_agent_context()
            return SubAgentResult(
                project_id=project_id,
                worker_id=worker_id,
                goal=task_description,
                success=False,
                failure_reason=e.failure_reason,
                error_message=str(e),
                created_artifacts=self._build_created_artifacts_map(state),
                summary=self._generate_failure_summary(state, str(e)),
                unplanned_creations=state.unplanned_creations,
                duration_seconds=(datetime.utcnow() - start).total_seconds(),
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.exception(f"[worker {worker_number}] Unexpected failure: {e}")
            clear_agent_context()
            return SubAgentResult(
                project_id=project_id,
                worker_id=worker_id,
                goal=task_description,
                success=False,
                failure_reason=SubAgentFailureReason.INTERNAL_ERROR,
                error_message=str(e),
                created_artifacts=self._build_created_artifacts_map(state),
                summary=self._generate_failure_summary(state, str(e)),
                unplanned_creations=state.unplanned_creations,
                duration_seconds=(datetime.utcnow() - start).total_seconds(),
            )

        duration = (datetime.utcnow() - start).total_seconds()

        if state.is_complete and state.created_artifact_id:
            logger.info(
                f"[worker {worker_number}] Done: {state.created_artifact_id} "
                f"in {state.step_count} steps ({duration:.1f}s)"
            )
            clear_agent_context()
            return SubAgentResult(
                project_id=project_id,
                worker_id=worker_id,
                goal=task_description,
                success=True,
                created_artifacts={
                    **state.coordinated_artifacts,
                    temp_id: state.created_artifact_id,
                },
                summary=self._generate_summary(state),
                context={
                    "entity_type": entity_type,
                    "artifact_id": state.created_artifact_id,
                    "common_name": state.generated_common_name,
                },
                unplanned_creations=state.unplanned_creations,
                duration_seconds=duration,
            )
        else:
            error_msg = state.errors[-1] if state.errors else "No artifact created"
            logger.error(f"[worker {worker_number}] Failed: {error_msg}")
            clear_agent_context()
            return SubAgentResult(
                project_id=project_id,
                worker_id=worker_id,
                goal=task_description,
                success=False,
                failure_reason=SubAgentFailureReason.PLAN_FAILED,
                error_message=error_msg,
                created_artifacts=self._build_created_artifacts_map(state),
                summary=self._generate_failure_summary(state, error_msg),
                unplanned_creations=state.unplanned_creations,
                duration_seconds=duration,
            )

    # ========================================================================
    # INITIALIZATION
    # ========================================================================

    async def _initialize(
        self,
        state: WorkerState,
        dependency_temp_ids: List[str],
    ) -> None:
        """
        Load context from Blackboard BEFORE the ReAct loop starts.
        The LLM will see all of this on its very first reasoning call.
        """
        logger.info(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Initializing context...")

        # If this execution is a retry/update of an existing artifact, pre-register
        # temp_id mapping so nested retries resolve to the same artifact ID.
        if state.target_artifact_id:
            self.blackboard.register_temp_id(state.temp_id, state.target_artifact_id)

        # 1. Register agent memory in Blackboard
        self.blackboard.create_agent_memory(
            agent_id=state.worker_id,
            task=state.task_description,
            entity_type=state.entity_type,
            parent_id=state.parent_worker_id,
        )

        # 2. Load accumulated facts (context from previous workers)
        state.facts_summary = self.blackboard.get_facts_summary(max_facts=30)

        # 3. Load dependency summaries — what previous workers discovered
        #    about the artifacts this worker's entity depends on
        state.dependency_summaries = self.blackboard.get_summaries_for_dependencies(
            dependency_temp_ids
        )

        logger.info(
            f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Loaded {len(state.dependency_summaries)} "
            f"dependency summaries"
        )

        # 4. Extract and inject constraints proactively (§5.5 ablation-gated)
        ablation = getattr(self.blackboard, 'ablation_config', None)
        enable_constraints = ablation.constraint_injection if ablation else True
        if enable_constraints and self.blackboard.model_code:
            try:
                from .constraint_extractor import ConstraintExtractor

                extractor = ConstraintExtractor(self.blackboard.model_code)
                constraints = extractor.extract_all_constraints()

                # Filter constraints for this entity type
                entity_constraints = [
                    c for c in constraints if c.entity_name == state.entity_type
                ]

                if entity_constraints:
                    lines = [f"## Constraints for {state.entity_type}", ""]
                    for c in entity_constraints:
                        severity_icon = {
                            "error": "❌",
                            "warning": "⚠️",
                            "info": "ℹ️",
                        }.get(c.severity, "•")
                        lines.append(f"{severity_icon} {c.description}")
                    state.entity_constraints = "\n".join(lines)
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Extracted {len(entity_constraints)} constraints"
                    )
            except Exception as e:
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Constraint extraction failed: {e}"
                )

        # 4b. Merge runtime-learned constraints from Blackboard
        #     These are accumulated by earlier workers that hit validation errors
        #     on the same entity type during this session.
        runtime_constraints = [
            c
            for c in self.blackboard.get_constraints(state.entity_type)
            if c.get("source") == "runtime_learning"
        ]
        if runtime_constraints:
            runtime_lines = ["\n## Runtime-Learned Constraints (from earlier workers):"]
            for rc in runtime_constraints:
                runtime_lines.append(f"⚠️  {rc.get('rule', '')}")
            runtime_section = "\n".join(runtime_lines)
            state.entity_constraints = (
                state.entity_constraints or ""
            ) + runtime_section
            logger.info(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Loaded {len(runtime_constraints)} "
                f"runtime-learned constraint(s) for {state.entity_type}"
            )

        # 5. Load circular reference fields from Blackboard classification
        #    These fields require two-phase commit: create artifact first,
        #    then the orchestrator patches them after the referenced artifact exists.
        try:
            state.circular_ref_fields = self.blackboard.get_circular_fields(
                state.entity_type
            )
            if state.circular_ref_fields:
                logger.info(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] CIRCULAR ref fields for "
                    f"{state.entity_type}: {state.circular_ref_fields}"
                )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Could not load circular ref fields: {e}"
            )

        # 6. Fetch model schema for all workers (top-tier and subworkers alike).
        #    This is a transparent pre-fetch done before the LLM loop starts,
        #    so it never costs an LLM tool-call.  The schema is injected directly
        #    into the system prompt via schema_section.
        try:
            schema_result = await self.mcp.call_tool(
                "get_model_schema",
                {"model_name": state.entity_type, "project_id": state.project_id},
            )
            if schema_result.get("success"):
                state.model_schema = schema_result.get("schema")
                logger.info(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Loaded model schema for {state.entity_type}"
                )
            else:
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Could not load schema: "
                    f"{schema_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
                )
        except Exception as e:

content.thinking: Let me continue reading the worker_agent.py and also start reading the other files in parallel.

content.text:
logger.warning(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Failed to fetch model schema: {e}"
            )

        # 7. RAG context retrieval — domain knowledge from the RAG service
        #    Queries with task description + entity type to get relevant examples,
        #    guidelines, and best practices. Ablation-gated via rag_context flag.
        enable_rag = ablation.rag_context if ablation else True
        if enable_rag:
            try:
                from rag_client import RAGClient

                rag = RAGClient()
                if rag.rag_enabled:
                    query = f"{state.entity_type}: {state.task_description}"
                    results = await rag.search(
                        query=query,
                        collection="documents",
                        top_k=3,
                        score_threshold=0.30,
                    )
                    if results:
                        lines = ["DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE (from knowledge base):"]
                        for r in results:
                            content = r.get("content", r.get("text", ""))
                            score = r.get("score", 0.0)
                            topic = r.get("metadata", {}).get("topic", "")
                            label = f" [{topic}]" if topic else ""
                            lines.append(f"  ({score:.0%}{label}) {content[:500]}")
                        state.rag_context = "\n".join(lines)
                        logger.info(
                            f"[worker {state.worker_number}] RAG: {len(results)} "
                            f"context chunks retrieved"
                        )
            except Exception as e:
                logger.debug(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] RAG retrieval skipped: {e}"
                )

    # ========================================================================
    # REACT LOOP — THE CORE OF THE WORKER
    # ========================================================================

    async def _react_loop(self, state: WorkerState) -> None:
        """
        Genuine Reason → Act → Observe loop.

        The LLM sees:
          - Its task and constraints (system prompt, constant)
          - Full tool call history (conversation, grows each step)

        The LLM outputs:
          - {"tool": "tool_name", "args": {...}}   → execute tool
          - {"tool": "finish", "args": {}}         → done successfully
          - {"tool": "fail", "args": {"reason": "..."}} → done with error
        """
        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(state)

        logger.info(
            f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Starting ReAct loop (max {MAX_STEPS} steps)..."
        )

        while state.step_count < MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.step_count += 1

            # §4.5 Roll up older steps when history grows long (§5.5 ablation-gated)
            _abl_cfg = getattr(self.blackboard, 'ablation_config', None)
            if _abl_cfg is None or _abl_cfg.context_compression:
                self._maybe_compress_history(state)

            # ── REASON: ask LLM what to do next ──────────────────────
            messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, state)

            try:
                response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                content = (
                    response.get("content", "")
                    if isinstance(response, dict)
                    else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                )
            except Exception as e:
                # Retry up to 2 times on transient network/timeout errors
                _retries = 2
                _succeeded = False
                for _attempt in range(1, _retries + 1):
                    logger.warning(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed (attempt {_attempt}/{_retries}): {e}"
                    )
                    import asyncio as _aio
                    await _aio.sleep(min(2 ** _attempt, 10))
                    try:
                        response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                        content = (
                            response.get("content", "")
                            if isinstance(response, dict)
                            else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                        )
                        _succeeded = True
                        break
                    except Exception as retry_e:
                        e = retry_e
                if not _succeeded:
                    state.errors.append(f"LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    break

            # ── PARSE: extract tool call from LLM response ───────────
            action = self._parse_action(content)
            if action is None:
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Could not parse action from: "
                    f"{content[:200]}"
                )
                state.errors.append("Failed to parse LLM action")
                break

            # ── HANDLE finish/fail ────────────────────────────────────
            if action["tool"] == "finish":
                if state.created_artifact_id:
                    await self._complete(state)
                else:
                    state.errors.append("LLM called finish but no artifact was created")
                break

            if action["tool"] == "fail":
                reason = action.get("args", {}).get("reason", "Worker failed")
                state.errors.append(reason)
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM called fail: {reason}"
                )
                break

            # ── ACT: execute the tool call ────────────────────────────
            tool_name = action["tool"]
            tool_args = action.get("args", {})

            # Inject project_id automatically — LLM doesn't need to know it
            tool_args["project_id"] = state.project_id

            # Inject pre-assigned artifact_id for create_artifact calls
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and state.planned_artifact_id
                and not tool_args.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                tool_args["artifact_id"] = state.planned_artifact_id

            # Ensure create_artifact has a stable user-facing handle unless the LLM already set one.
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and not tool_args.get("common_name"):
                generated = self._derive_default_common_name(state)
                state.generated_common_name = generated
                tool_args["common_name"] = generated

            # Inject recursion_depth and parent_worker_id for spawn_worker
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker":
                tool_args["recursion_depth"] = state.recursion_depth
                tool_args["parent_worker_id"] = state.worker_id
                if not tool_args.get("target_artifact_id"):
                    existing_child_id = self.blackboard.resolve_temp_id(
                        tool_args.get("temp_id", "")
                    )
                    if existing_child_id:
                        tool_args["target_artifact_id"] = existing_child_id

            # Resolve any temp_ids in args to real artifact_ids
            tool_args = await self._resolve_temp_ids(tool_args, state)

            logger.info(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Step {state.step_count}: "
                f"{tool_name}({', '.join(f'{k}={v}' for k, v in list(tool_args.items())[:3])}...)"
            )

            try:
                result = await self.mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Tool call failed: {e}")
                result = {"success": False, "error": str(e)}

            # ── OBSERVE: record result for next LLM call ─────────────
            tc = ToolCall(
                tool_name=tool_name,
                args=tool_args,
                result=result,
                success=result.get("success", True),
            )
            state.tool_call_history.append(tc)

            # Emit tool_update event for graph visualizer
            if self._event_callback:
                try:
                    await self._event_callback(
                        "tool_update",
                        {
                            "worker_id": state.worker_id,
                            "temp_id": state.temp_id,
                            "tools": [
                                {
                                    "name": tc.tool_name,
                                    "status": "success" if tc.success else "failed",
                                    "arguments": tc.args,
                                    "result": self._truncate_result(tc.result),
                                    "timestamp": tc.timestamp.isoformat(),
                                }
                            ],
                        },
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.debug(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Failed to emit tool_update: {e}"
                    )

            # Track the primary artifact_id when create_artifact succeeds
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and result.get("success")
                and result.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                created_model = tool_args.get("model_name", "")
                artifact_id = result["artifact_id"]

                if created_model == state.entity_type:
                    # This is the planned primary artifact for this worker
                    state.created_artifact_id = artifact_id
                    self.blackboard.register_temp_id(state.temp_id, artifact_id)
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Artifact created: {artifact_id}"
                    )
                else:
                    # Off-type creation — not part of the orchestrator's plan.
                    common_name = (
                        tool_args.get("common_name", "")
                        or f"{created_model.lower()}_for_{state.temp_id}"
                    )
                    state.unplanned_creations.append(
                        UnplannedArtifact(
                            artifact_id=artifact_id,
                            model_name=created_model,
                            common_name=common_name,
                            created_by_worker_id=state.worker_id,
                            reason=f"created inline by worker for {state.entity_type} ({state.temp_id})",
                        )
                    )
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Unplanned {created_model} "
                        f"created: {artifact_id} (common_name={common_name!r})"
                    )

            # Collect unplanned creations bubbled up from spawn_worker children
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker" and result.get("success"):
                for child_temp_id, child_artifact_id in (
                    result.get("created_artifacts", {}) or {}
                ).items():
                    if isinstance(child_temp_id, str) and isinstance(child_artifact_id, str):
                        state.coordinated_artifacts[child_temp_id] = child_artifact_id
                child_unplanned = result.get("unplanned_creations", [])
                for item in child_unplanned:
                    if isinstance(item, dict):
                        try:
                            state.unplanned_creations.append(UnplannedArtifact(**item))
                        except Exception:
                            pass
                    elif isinstance(item, UnplannedArtifact):
                        state.unplanned_creations.append(item)

            # Handle validation failures — inject HITL if exhausted
            if tool_name == "validate_artifact" and result.get("valid") is False:
                await self._handle_validation_failure(state, result)

        if state.step_count >= MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.errors.append(f"MAX_STEPS ({MAX_STEPS}) exceeded")
            logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Max steps exceeded")

    # ========================================================================
    # LLM PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ========================================================================

    def _build_system_prompt(self, state: WorkerState) -> str:
        """
        System prompt — compact version optimised for token efficiency.
        ~60% smaller than the verbose version while preserving all critical rules.
        """
        # ── Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────
        dependency_section = ""
        if state.dependency_summaries:
            dep_lines = []
            for dep in state.dependency_summaries:
                dep_lines.append(
                    f"  - {dep['entity_type']} [{dep['artifact_id']}]: {dep['summary']}"
                )
            dependency_section = (
                "DEPENDENCIES (use these artifact IDs for reference fields):\n"
                + "\n".join(dep_lines)
            )

        # ── Hints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        hints_section = ""
        if state.supervisor_hints:
            hints_section = "HUMAN GUIDANCE:\n" + "\n".join(
                f"  - {h}" for h in state.supervisor_hints
            )

        # ── Subsystem scope ───────────────────────────────────────────
        scope_section = ""
        if state.assigned_classes:
            class_list = ", ".join(
                ac.get("class") or ac.get("model_name", "")
                for ac in state.assigned_classes
            )
            scope_section = (
                f"SUBSYSTEM SCOPE: Only create_artifact for: {class_list}\n"
                f"Use spawn_worker for out-of-scope dependency types."
            )

        # ── Interface contracts ───────────────────────────────────────
        contracts_section = ""
        if state.parent_context:
            c_lines = []
            for temp_id, meta in state.parent_context.items():
                c_lines.append(
                    f"  - {meta.get('model_name','?')} [{meta.get('artifact_id','pending')}] "
                    f"from '{meta.get('subsystem_id','?')}': {meta.get('intent','')}"
                )
            contracts_section = (
                "SHARED RESOURCES (from parent subsystems — use these IDs, do NOT recreate):\n"
                + "\n".join(c_lines)
            )

        # ── Circular refs ─────────────────────────────────────────────
        circular_section = ""
        if state.circular_ref_fields:
            fields_str = ", ".join(state.circular_ref_fields)
            circular_section = (
                f"CIRCULAR FIELDS ({fields_str}): Skip if target doesn't exist yet. "
                f"The orchestrator patches them automatically."
            )

        # ── Schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        schema_section = ""
        if state.model_schema:
            props = state.model_schema.get("properties", {})
            required = state.model_schema.get("required", [])
            f_lines = []
            for fname, finfo in props.items():
                req = "*" if fname in required else " "
                ftype = finfo.get("type", "?")
                tool = finfo.get("use_tool", "set_field")
                ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
                enum_vals = finfo.get("enum_values", [])

                extra = ""
                if ref_target:
                    extra = f" -> {ref_target}"
                elif enum_vals:
                    extra = f" [{', '.join(str(v) for v in enum_vals[:8])}]"

                alias = finfo.get("alias")
                alias_note = f"|{alias}" if alias else ""
                f_lines.append(f"  {req} {fname}{alias_note}: {ftype}{extra} ({tool})")

            schema_section = (
                f"SCHEMA {state.entity_type} (* = required):\n"
                + "\n".join(f_lines)
            )

        # ── Mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if state.target_artifact_id:
            mode_line = f"Mode: UPDATE artifact {state.target_artifact_id}. Do NOT call create_artifact."
            mode_flow = "get_artifact -> batch_set_fields/set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"
        else:
            mode_line = f"Mode: CREATE new {state.entity_type}."
            mode_flow = "create_artifact (once) -> batch_set_fields + set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"

        # ── RAG context ───────────────────────────────────────────
        rag_section = state.rag_context or ""

        return f"""Worker Agent. {mode_line}

TASK: {state.task_description}

{dependency_section}
{state.entity_constraints or ""}
{hints_section}
{rag_section}
{scope_section}
{contracts_section}
{circular_section}
{schema_section}

TOOLS (all take artifact_id as UUID, orchestrator resolves temp_ids automatically):

    # ========================================================================
    # §5.1 METAMODEL-GUIDED STEP PLANNER — lean status block
    # ========================================================================

    def _compute_metamodel_status(self, state: "WorkerState") -> str:
        """
        Deterministic computation of what the worker has done vs. what remains,
        based on the metamodel schema and tool call history.  Zero LLM calls.

        Returns a compact status string (~100 tokens) injected before each
        ReAct iteration so the LLM knows WHERE it is in the metamodel graph.
        """
        if not state.model_schema:
            return ""

        props = state.model_schema.get("properties", {})
        required = set(state.model_schema.get("required", []))

        # Scan tool call history to determine which fields have been set
        fields_set: set = set()
        artifact_created = state.created_artifact_id is not None

        for tc in state.tool_call_history:
            if not tc.success:
                continue
            if tc.tool_name == "batch_set_fields":
                try:
                    fkeys = json.loads(tc.args.get("fields_json", "{}")).keys()
                    fields_set.update(fkeys)
                except Exception:
                    pass
            elif tc.tool_name == "set_reference":
                fname = tc.args.get("field_name", "")
                if fname:
                    fields_set.add(fname)
            elif tc.tool_name == "append_to_collection":
                fname = tc.args.get("field_name", "")
                if fname:
                    fields_set.add(fname)

        # Determine remaining required fields
        remaining = []
        for fname, finfo in props.items():
            if fname in fields_set:
                continue
            is_req = fname in required
            use_tool = finfo.get("use_tool", "set_field")
            ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
            # Skip derived fields — reconciler handles them
            ref_class = ""
            if self.blackboard._reference_classification:
                ref_info = self.blackboard._reference_classification.get(
                    state.entity_type, {}
                ).get(fname, {})
                if isinstance(ref_info, dict):
                    ref_class = ref_info.get("ref_class", "")
            if ref_class == "derived":
                continue
            # Skip circular fields if target doesn't exist yet
            if ref_class == "circular" and fname in state.circular_ref_fields:
                continue

            tag = "REQ" if is_req else "opt"
            type_hint = f"->{ref_target}" if ref_target else finfo.get("type", "")
            remaining.append(f"{fname}({tag},{type_hint})")

        ref_cls = self.blackboard._reference_classification or {}
        entity_refs = ref_cls.get(state.entity_type, {})
        total_settable = len([
            f for f in props
            if entity_refs.get(f, {}).get("ref_class") != "derived"
        ])
        done_count = total_settable - len(remaining)
        pct = done_count / max(total_settable, 1)

        lines = []
        if not artifact_created:
            lines.append("STATUS: No artifact created yet. Call create_artifact first.")
        else:
            lines.append(f"STATUS: {done_count}/{total_settable} fields done ({pct:.0%})")
            if remaining:
                lines.append(f"  Remaining: {', '.join(remaining[:12])}")
                if len(remaining) > 12:
                    lines.append(f"  ... and {len(remaining) - 12} more")
            else:
                lines.append("  All fields set. Validate and commit.")

    # ========================================================================
    # REACT LOOP — THE CORE OF THE WORKER
    # ========================================================================

    async def _react_loop(self, state: WorkerState) -> None:
        """
        Genuine Reason → Act → Observe loop.

        The LLM sees:
          - Its task and constraints (system prompt, constant)
          - Full tool call history (conversation, grows each step)

        The LLM outputs:
          - {"tool": "tool_name", "args": {...}}   → execute tool
          - {"tool": "finish", "args": {}}         → done successfully
          - {"tool": "fail", "args": {"reason": "..."}} → done with error
        """
        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(state)

        logger.info(
            f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Starting ReAct loop (max {MAX_STEPS} steps)..."
        )

        while state.step_count < MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.step_count += 1

            # §4.5 Roll up older steps when history grows long (§5.5 ablation-gated)
            _abl_cfg = getattr(self.blackboard, 'ablation_config', None)
            if _abl_cfg is None or _abl_cfg.context_compression:
                self._maybe_compress_history(state)

            # ── REASON: ask LLM what to do next ──────────────────────
            messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, state)

            try:
                response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                content = (
                    response.get("content", "")
                    if isinstance(response, dict)
                    else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                )
            except Exception as e:
                # Retry up to 2 times on transient network/timeout errors
                _retries = 2
                _succeeded = False
                for _attempt in range(1, _retries + 1):
                    logger.warning(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed (attempt {_attempt}/{_retries}): {e}"
                    )
                    import asyncio as _aio
                    await _aio.sleep(min(2 ** _attempt, 10))
                    try:
                        response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                        content = (
                            response.get("content", "")
                            if isinstance(response, dict)
                            else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                        )
                        _succeeded = True
                        break
                    except Exception as retry_e:
                        e = retry_e
                if not _succeeded:
                    state.errors.append(f"LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    break

            # ── PARSE: extract tool call from LLM response ───────────
            action = self._parse_action(content)
            if action is None:
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Could not parse action from: "
                    f"{content[:200]}"
                )
                state.errors.append("Failed to parse LLM action")
                break

            # ── HANDLE finish/fail ────────────────────────────────────
            if action["tool"] == "finish":
                if state.created_artifact_id:
                    await self._complete(state)
                else:
                    state.errors.append("LLM called finish but no artifact was created")
                break

            if action["tool"] == "fail":
                reason = action.get("args", {}).get("reason", "Worker failed")
                state.errors.append(reason)
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM called fail: {reason}"
                )
                break

            # ── ACT: execute the tool call ────────────────────────────
            tool_name = action["tool"]
            tool_args = action.get("args", {})

            # Inject project_id automatically — LLM doesn't need to know it
            tool_args["project_id"] = state.project_id

            # Inject pre-assigned artifact_id for create_artifact calls
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and state.planned_artifact_id
                and not tool_args.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                tool_args["artifact_id"] = state.planned_artifact_id

            # Ensure create_artifact has a stable user-facing handle unless the LLM already set one.
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and not tool_args.get("common_name"):
                generated = self._derive_default_common_name(state)
                state.generated_common_name = generated
                tool_args["common_name"] = generated

            # Inject recursion_depth and parent_worker_id for spawn_worker
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker":
                tool_args["recursion_depth"] = state.recursion_depth
                tool_args["parent_worker_id"] = state.worker_id
                if not tool_args.get("target_artifact_id"):
                    existing_child_id = self.blackboard.resolve_temp_id(
                        tool_args.get("temp_id", "")
                    )
                    if existing_child_id:
                        tool_args["target_artifact_id"] = existing_child_id

            # Resolve any temp_ids in args to real artifact_ids
            tool_args = await self._resolve_temp_ids(tool_args, state)

            logger.info(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Step {state.step_count}: "
                f"{tool_name}({', '.join(f'{k}={v}' for k, v in list(tool_args.items())[:3])}...)"
            )

            try:
                result = await self.mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Tool call failed: {e}")
                result = {"success": False, "error": str(e)}

            # ── OBSERVE: record result for next LLM call ─────────────
            tc = ToolCall(
                tool_name=tool_name,
                args=tool_args,
                result=result,
                success=result.get("success", True),
            )
            state.tool_call_history.append(tc)

            # Emit tool_update event for graph visualizer
            if self._event_callback:
                try:
                    await self._event_callback(
                        "tool_update",
                        {
                            "worker_id": state.worker_id,
                            "temp_id": state.temp_id,
                            "tools": [
                                {
                                    "name": tc.tool_name,
                                    "status": "success" if tc.success else "failed",
                                    "arguments": tc.args,
                                    "result": self._truncate_result(tc.result),
                                    "timestamp": tc.timestamp.isoformat(),
                                }
                            ],
                        },
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.debug(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Failed to emit tool_update: {e}"
                    )

            # Track the primary artifact_id when create_artifact succeeds
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and result.get("success")
                and result.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                created_model = tool_args.get("model_name", "")
                artifact_id = result["artifact_id"]

                if created_model == state.entity_type:
                    # This is the planned primary artifact for this worker
                    state.created_artifact_id = artifact_id
                    self.blackboard.register_temp_id(state.temp_id, artifact_id)
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Artifact created: {artifact_id}"
                    )
                else:
                    # Off-type creation — not part of the orchestrator's plan.
                    common_name = (
                        tool_args.get("common_name", "")
                        or f"{created_model.lower()}_for_{state.temp_id}"
                    )
                    state.unplanned_creations.append(
                        UnplannedArtifact(
                            artifact_id=artifact_id,
                            model_name=created_model,
                            common_name=common_name,
                            created_by_worker_id=state.worker_id,
                            reason=f"created inline by worker for {state.entity_type} ({state.temp_id})",
                        )
                    )
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Unplanned {created_model} "
                        f"created: {artifact_id} (common_name={common_name!r})"
                    )

            # Collect unplanned creations bubbled up from spawn_worker children
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker" and result.get("success"):
                for child_temp_id, child_artifact_id in (
                    result.get("created_artifacts", {}) or {}
                ).items():
                    if isinstance(child_temp_id, str) and isinstance(child_artifact_id, str):
                        state.coordinated_artifacts[child_temp_id] = child_artifact_id
                child_unplanned = result.get("unplanned_creations", [])
                for item in child_unplanned:
                    if isinstance(item, dict):
                        try:
                            state.unplanned_creations.append(UnplannedArtifact(**item))
                        except Exception:
                            pass
                    elif isinstance(item, UnplannedArtifact):
                        state.unplanned_creations.append(item)

            # Handle validation failures — inject HITL if exhausted
            if tool_name == "validate_artifact" and result.get("valid") is False:
                await self._handle_validation_failure(state, result)

        if state.step_count >= MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.errors.append(f"MAX_STEPS ({MAX_STEPS}) exceeded")
            logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Max steps exceeded")

    # ========================================================================
    # LLM PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ========================================================================

    def _build_system_prompt(self, state: WorkerState) -> str:
        """
        System prompt — compact version optimised for token efficiency.
        ~60% smaller than the verbose version while preserving all critical rules.
        """
        # ── Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────
        dependency_section = ""
        if state.dependency_summaries:
            dep_lines = []
            for dep in state.dependency_summaries:
                dep_lines.append(
                    f"  - {dep['entity_type']} [{dep['artifact_id']}]: {dep['summary']}"
                )
            dependency_section = (
                "DEPENDENCIES (use these artifact IDs for reference fields):\n"
                + "\n".join(dep_lines)
            )

        # ── Hints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        hints_section = ""
        if state.supervisor_hints:
            hints_section = "HUMAN GUIDANCE:\n" + "\n".join(
                f"  - {h}" for h in state.supervisor_hints
            )

        # ── Subsystem scope ───────────────────────────────────────────
        scope_section = ""
        if state.assigned_classes:
            class_list = ", ".join(
                ac.get("class") or ac.get("model_name", "")
                for ac in state.assigned_classes
            )
            scope_section = (
                f"SUBSYSTEM SCOPE: Only create_artifact for: {class_list}\n"
                f"Use spawn_worker for out-of-scope dependency types."
            )

        # ── Interface contracts ───────────────────────────────────────
        contracts_section = ""
        if state.parent_context:
            c_lines = []
            for temp_id, meta in state.parent_context.items():
                c_lines.append(
                    f"  - {meta.get('model_name','?')} [{meta.get('artifact_id','pending')}] "
                    f"from '{meta.get('subsystem_id','?')}': {meta.get('intent','')}"
                )
            contracts_section = (
                "SHARED RESOURCES (from parent subsystems — use these IDs, do NOT recreate):\n"
                + "\n".join(c_lines)
            )

        # ── Circular refs ─────────────────────────────────────────────
        circular_section = ""
        if state.circular_ref_fields:
            fields_str = ", ".join(state.circular_ref_fields)
            circular_section = (
                f"CIRCULAR FIELDS ({fields_str}): Skip if target doesn't exist yet. "
                f"The orchestrator patches them automatically."
            )

        # ── Schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        schema_section = ""
        if state.model_schema:
            props = state.model_schema.get("properties", {})
            required = state.model_schema.get("required", [])
            f_lines = []
            for fname, finfo in props.items():
                req = "*" if fname in required else " "
                ftype = finfo.get("type", "?")
                tool = finfo.get("use_tool", "set_field")
                ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
                enum_vals = finfo.get("enum_values", [])

                extra = ""
                if ref_target:
                    extra = f" -> {ref_target}"
                elif enum_vals:
                    extra = f" [{', '.join(str(v) for v in enum_vals[:8])}]"

                alias = finfo.get("alias")
                alias_note = f"|{alias}" if alias else ""
                f_lines.append(f"  {req} {fname}{alias_note}: {ftype}{extra} ({tool})")

            schema_section = (
                f"SCHEMA {state.entity_type} (* = required):\n"
                + "\n".join(f_lines)
            )

        # ── Mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if state.target_artifact_id:
            mode_line = f"Mode: UPDATE artifact {state.target_artifact_id}. Do NOT call create_artifact."
            mode_flow = "get_artifact -> batch_set_fields/set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"
        else:
            mode_line = f"Mode: CREATE new {state.entity_type}."
            mode_flow = "create_artifact (once) -> batch_set_fields + set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"

        # ── RAG context ───────────────────────────────────────────
        rag_section = state.rag_context or ""

        return f"""Worker Agent. {mode_line}

TASK: {state.task_description}

{dependency_section}
{state.entity_constraints or ""}
{hints_section}
{rag_section}
{scope_section}
{contracts_section}
{circular_section}
{schema_section}

TOOLS (all take artifact_id as UUID, orchestrator resolves temp_ids automatically):
  Artifact: query_artifacts(model_name) | get_artifact(artifact_id) | create_artifact(model_name, common_name?) | get_model_schema(model_name)
  Fields:   batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json) — scalars only, as JSON string
  Refs:     set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value) — single ref
            append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value) — list ref
  Validate: validate_artifact(artifact_id) | commit_artifact(artifact_id)
  Graph:    query_model_graph(entity_type) | get_schema_for(entity_type) | find_candidates_for_field(artifact_id, field_name)
  Bus:      publish_to_blackboard(topic, message_json) | check_blackboard_topic(topic, since_seq?)
  Code:     execute_sandboxed_code(code, language?) — isolated sandbox, save files to /workspace/shared/
  Delegate: spawn_worker(goal, entity_type, temp_id, dependency_temp_ids, target_artifact_id?) — depth {state.recursion_depth}/{MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH}

                )

    # ========================================================================
    # REACT LOOP — THE CORE OF THE WORKER
    # ========================================================================

    async def _react_loop(self, state: WorkerState) -> None:
        """
        Genuine Reason → Act → Observe loop.

        The LLM sees:
          - Its task and constraints (system prompt, constant)
          - Full tool call history (conversation, grows each step)

        The LLM outputs:
          - {"tool": "tool_name", "args": {...}}   → execute tool
          - {"tool": "finish", "args": {}}         → done successfully
          - {"tool": "fail", "args": {"reason": "..."}} → done with error
        """
        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(state)

        logger.info(
            f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Starting ReAct loop (max {MAX_STEPS} steps)..."
        )

        while state.step_count < MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.step_count += 1

            # §4.5 Roll up older steps when history grows long (§5.5 ablation-gated)
            _abl_cfg = getattr(self.blackboard, 'ablation_config', None)
            if _abl_cfg is None or _abl_cfg.context_compression:
                self._maybe_compress_history(state)

            # ── REASON: ask LLM what to do next ──────────────────────
            messages = self._build_messages(system_prompt, state)

            try:
                response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                content = (
                    response.get("content", "")
                    if isinstance(response, dict)
                    else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                )
            except Exception as e:
                # Retry up to 2 times on transient network/timeout errors
                _retries = 2
                _succeeded = False
                for _attempt in range(1, _retries + 1):
                    logger.warning(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed (attempt {_attempt}/{_retries}): {e}"
                    )
                    import asyncio as _aio
                    await _aio.sleep(min(2 ** _attempt, 10))
                    try:
                        response = await self.llm.chat(messages)
                        content = (
                            response.get("content", "")
                            if isinstance(response, dict)
                            else getattr(response, "content", str(response))
                        )
                        _succeeded = True
                        break
                    except Exception as retry_e:
                        e = retry_e
                if not _succeeded:
                    state.errors.append(f"LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM call failed after retries: {e}")
                    break

            # ── PARSE: extract tool call from LLM response ───────────
            action = self._parse_action(content)
            if action is None:
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Could not parse action from: "
                    f"{content[:200]}"
                )
                state.errors.append("Failed to parse LLM action")
                break

            # ── HANDLE finish/fail ────────────────────────────────────
            if action["tool"] == "finish":
                if state.created_artifact_id:
                    await self._complete(state)
                else:
                    state.errors.append("LLM called finish but no artifact was created")
                break

            if action["tool"] == "fail":
                reason = action.get("args", {}).get("reason", "Worker failed")
                state.errors.append(reason)
                logger.warning(
                    f"[worker {state.worker_number}] LLM called fail: {reason}"
                )
                break

            # ── ACT: execute the tool call ────────────────────────────
            tool_name = action["tool"]
            tool_args = action.get("args", {})

            # Inject project_id automatically — LLM doesn't need to know it
            tool_args["project_id"] = state.project_id

            # Inject pre-assigned artifact_id for create_artifact calls
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and state.planned_artifact_id
                and not tool_args.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                tool_args["artifact_id"] = state.planned_artifact_id

            # Ensure create_artifact has a stable user-facing handle unless the LLM already set one.
            if tool_name == "create_artifact" and not tool_args.get("common_name"):
                generated = self._derive_default_common_name(state)
                state.generated_common_name = generated
                tool_args["common_name"] = generated

            # Inject recursion_depth and parent_worker_id for spawn_worker
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker":
                tool_args["recursion_depth"] = state.recursion_depth
                tool_args["parent_worker_id"] = state.worker_id
                if not tool_args.get("target_artifact_id"):
                    existing_child_id = self.blackboard.resolve_temp_id(
                        tool_args.get("temp_id", "")
                    )
                    if existing_child_id:
                        tool_args["target_artifact_id"] = existing_child_id

            # Resolve any temp_ids in args to real artifact_ids
            tool_args = await self._resolve_temp_ids(tool_args, state)

            logger.info(
                f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Step {state.step_count}: "
                f"{tool_name}({', '.join(f'{k}={v}' for k, v in list(tool_args.items())[:3])}...)"
            )

            try:
                result = await self.mcp.call_tool(tool_name, tool_args)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Tool call failed: {e}")
                result = {"success": False, "error": str(e)}

            # ── OBSERVE: record result for next LLM call ─────────────
            tc = ToolCall(
                tool_name=tool_name,
                args=tool_args,
                result=result,
                success=result.get("success", True),
            )
            state.tool_call_history.append(tc)

            # Emit tool_update event for graph visualizer
            if self._event_callback:
                try:
                    await self._event_callback(
                        "tool_update",
                        {
                            "worker_id": state.worker_id,
                            "temp_id": state.temp_id,
                            "tools": [
                                {
                                    "name": tc.tool_name,
                                    "status": "success" if tc.success else "failed",
                                    "arguments": tc.args,
                                    "result": self._truncate_result(tc.result),
                                    "timestamp": tc.timestamp.isoformat(),
                                }
                            ],
                        },
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.debug(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Failed to emit tool_update: {e}"
                    )

            # Track the primary artifact_id when create_artifact succeeds
            if (
                tool_name == "create_artifact"
                and result.get("success")
                and result.get("artifact_id")
            ):
                created_model = tool_args.get("model_name", "")
                artifact_id = result["artifact_id"]

                if created_model == state.entity_type:
                    # This is the planned primary artifact for this worker
                    state.created_artifact_id = artifact_id
                    self.blackboard.register_temp_id(state.temp_id, artifact_id)
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Artifact created: {artifact_id}"
                    )
                else:
                    # Off-type creation — not part of the orchestrator's plan.
                    common_name = (
                        tool_args.get("common_name", "")
                        or f"{created_model.lower()}_for_{state.temp_id}"
                    )
                    state.unplanned_creations.append(
                        UnplannedArtifact(
                            artifact_id=artifact_id,
                            model_name=created_model,
                            common_name=common_name,
                            created_by_worker_id=state.worker_id,
                            reason=f"created inline by worker for {state.entity_type} ({state.temp_id})",
                        )
                    )
                    logger.info(
                        f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Unplanned {created_model} "
                        f"created: {artifact_id} (common_name={common_name!r})"
                    )

            # Collect unplanned creations bubbled up from spawn_worker children
            if tool_name == "spawn_worker" and result.get("success"):
                for child_temp_id, child_artifact_id in (
                    result.get("created_artifacts", {}) or {}
                ).items():
                    if isinstance(child_temp_id, str) and isinstance(child_artifact_id, str):
                        state.coordinated_artifacts[child_temp_id] = child_artifact_id
                child_unplanned = result.get("unplanned_creations", [])
                for item in child_unplanned:
                    if isinstance(item, dict):
                        try:
                            state.unplanned_creations.append(UnplannedArtifact(**item))
                        except Exception:
                            pass
                    elif isinstance(item, UnplannedArtifact):
                        state.unplanned_creations.append(item)

            # Handle validation failures — inject HITL if exhausted
            if tool_name == "validate_artifact" and result.get("valid") is False:
                await self._handle_validation_failure(state, result)

        if state.step_count >= MAX_STEPS and not state.is_complete:
            state.errors.append(f"MAX_STEPS ({MAX_STEPS}) exceeded")
            logger.error(f"[worker {state.worker_number}] Max steps exceeded")

    # ========================================================================
    # LLM PROMPT CONSTRUCTION
    # ========================================================================

    def _build_system_prompt(self, state: WorkerState) -> str:
        """
        System prompt — compact version optimised for token efficiency.
        ~60% smaller than the verbose version while preserving all critical rules.
        """
        # ── Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────
        dependency_section = ""
        if state.dependency_summaries:
            dep_lines = []
            for dep in state.dependency_summaries:
                dep_lines.append(
                    f"  - {dep['entity_type']} [{dep['artifact_id']}]: {dep['summary']}"
                )
            dependency_section = (
                "DEPENDENCIES (use these artifact IDs for reference fields):\n"
                + "\n".join(dep_lines)
            )

        # ── Hints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        hints_section = ""
        if state.supervisor_hints:
            hints_section = "HUMAN GUIDANCE:\n" + "\n".join(
                f"  - {h}" for h in state.supervisor_hints
            )

        # ── Subsystem scope ───────────────────────────────────────────
        scope_section = ""
        if state.assigned_classes:
            class_list = ", ".join(
                ac.get("class") or ac.get("model_name", "")
                for ac in state.assigned_classes
            )
            scope_section = (
                f"SUBSYSTEM SCOPE: Only create_artifact for: {class_list}\n"
                f"Use spawn_worker for out-of-scope dependency types."
            )

        # ── Interface contracts ───────────────────────────────────────
        contracts_section = ""
        if state.parent_context:
            c_lines = []
            for temp_id, meta in state.parent_context.items():
                c_lines.append(
                    f"  - {meta.get('model_name','?')} [{meta.get('artifact_id','pending')}] "
                    f"from '{meta.get('subsystem_id','?')}': {meta.get('intent','')}"
                )
            contracts_section = (
                "SHARED RESOURCES (from parent subsystems — use these IDs, do NOT recreate):\n"
                + "\n".join(c_lines)
            )

        # ── Circular refs ─────────────────────────────────────────────
        circular_section = ""
        if state.circular_ref_fields:
            fields_str = ", ".join(state.circular_ref_fields)
            circular_section = (
                f"CIRCULAR FIELDS ({fields_str}): Skip if target doesn't exist yet. "
                f"The orchestrator patches them automatically."
            )

        # ── Schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        schema_section = ""
        if state.model_schema:
            props = state.model_schema.get("properties", {})
            required = state.model_schema.get("required", [])
            f_lines = []
            for fname, finfo in props.items():
                req = "*" if fname in required else " "
                ftype = finfo.get("type", "?")
                tool = finfo.get("use_tool", "set_field")
                ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
                enum_vals = finfo.get("enum_values", [])

                extra = ""
                if ref_target:
                    extra = f" -> {ref_target}"
                elif enum_vals:
                    extra = f" [{', '.join(str(v) for v in enum_vals[:8])}]"

                alias = finfo.get("alias")
                alias_note = f"|{alias}" if alias else ""
                f_lines.append(f"  {req} {fname}{alias_note}: {ftype}{extra} ({tool})")

            schema_section = (
                f"SCHEMA {state.entity_type} (* = required):\n"
                + "\n".join(f_lines)
            )

        # ── Mode ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        if state.target_artifact_id:
            mode_line = f"Mode: UPDATE artifact {state.target_artifact_id}. Do NOT call create_artifact."
            mode_flow = "get_artifact -> batch_set_fields/set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"
        else:
            mode_line = f"Mode: CREATE new {state.entity_type}."
            mode_flow = "create_artifact (once) -> batch_set_fields + set_reference -> validate -> commit -> finish"

        # ── RAG context ───────────────────────────────────────────
        rag_section = state.rag_context or ""

        return f"""Worker Agent. {mode_line}

TASK: {state.task_description}

{dependency_section}
{state.entity_constraints or ""}
{hints_section}
{rag_section}
{scope_section}
{contracts_section}
{circular_section}
{schema_section}

TOOLS (all take artifact_id as UUID, orchestrator resolves temp_ids automatically):
  Artifact: query_artifacts(model_name) | get_artifact(artifact_id) | create_artifact(model_name, common_name?) | get_model_schema(model_name)
  Fields:   batch_set_fields(artifact_id, fields_json) — scalars only, as JSON string
  Refs:     set_reference(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value) — single ref
            append_to_collection(artifact_id, field_name, reference_value) — list ref
  Validate: validate_artifact(artifact_id) | commit_artifact(artifact_id)
  Graph:    query_model_graph(entity_type) | get_schema_for(entity_type) | find_candidates_for_field(artifact_id, field_name)
  Bus:      publish_to_blackboard(topic, message_json) | check_blackboard_topic(topic, since_seq?)
  Code:     execute_sandboxed_code(code, language?) — isolated sandbox, save files to /workspace/shared/
  Delegate: spawn_worker(goal, entity_type, temp_id, dependency_temp_ids, target_artifact_id?) — depth {state.recursion_depth}/{MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH}
  Control:  finish() | fail(reason)

RULES:
  - Schema is authority for field names. use_tool tells you which tool per field.
  - NEVER pass dicts/objects in batch_set_fields for reference fields — use set_reference/append_to_collection.
  - Flow: {mode_flow}
  - batch_set_fields: set ALL scalar fields in ONE call.
  - validate before commit. Fix errors if validation fails.
  - NEVER repeat identical tool calls.
  - For large data: use execute_sandboxed_code, save to /workspace/shared/, return only path+summary.
  - spawn_worker only for genuinely complex sub-entities.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON: {{"tool": "name", "args": {{...}}}}
"""

    # ========================================================================
    # §5.1 METAMODEL-GUIDED STEP PLANNER — lean status block
    # ========================================================================

    def _compute_metamodel_status(self, state: "WorkerState") -> str:
        """
        Deterministic computation of what the worker has done vs. what remains,
        based on the metamodel schema and tool call history.  Zero LLM calls.

        Returns a compact status string (~100 tokens) injected before each
        ReAct iteration so the LLM knows WHERE it is in the metamodel graph.
        """
        if not state.model_schema:
            return ""

        props = state.model_schema.get("properties", {})
        required = set(state.model_schema.get("required", []))

        # Scan tool call history to determine which fields have been set
        fields_set: set = set()
        artifact_created = state.created_artifact_id is not None

        for tc in state.tool_call_history:
            if not tc.success:
                continue
            if tc.tool_name == "batch_set_fields":
                try:
                    fkeys = json.loads(tc.args.get("fields_json", "{}")).keys()
                    fields_set.update(fkeys)
                except Exception:
                    pass
            elif tc.tool_name == "set_reference":
                fname = tc.args.get("field_name", "")
                if fname:
                    fields_set.add(fname)
            elif tc.tool_name == "append_to_collection":
                fname = tc.args.get("field_name", "")
                if fname:
                    fields_set.add(fname)

        # Determine remaining required fields
        remaining = []
        for fname, finfo in props.items():
            if fname in fields_set:
                continue
            is_req = fname in required
            use_tool = finfo.get("use_tool", "set_field")
            ref_target = finfo.get("reference_target", "")
            # Skip derived fields — reconciler handles them
            ref_class = ""
            if self.blackboard._reference_classification:
                ref_info = self.blackboard._reference_classification.get(
                    state.entity_type, {}
                ).get(fname, {})
                if isinstance(ref_info, dict):
                    ref_class = ref_info.get("ref_class", "")
            if ref_class == "derived":
                continue
            # Skip circular fields if target doesn't exist yet
            if ref_class == "circular" and fname in state.circular_ref_fields:
                continue

            tag = "REQ" if is_req else "opt"
            type_hint = f"->{ref_target}" if ref_target else finfo.get("type", "")
            remaining.append(f"{fname}({tag},{type_hint})")

        ref_cls = self.blackboard._reference_classification or {}
        entity_refs = ref_cls.get(state.entity_type, {})
        total_settable = len([
            f for f in props
            if entity_refs.get(f, {}).get("ref_class") != "derived"
        ])
        done_count = total_settable - len(remaining)
        pct = done_count / max(total_settable, 1)

        lines = []
        if not artifact_created:
            lines.append("STATUS: No artifact created yet. Call create_artifact first.")
        else:
            lines.append(f"STATUS: {done_count}/{total_settable} fields done ({pct:.0%})")
            if remaining:
                lines.append(f"  Remaining: {', '.join(remaining[:12])}")
                if len(remaining) > 12:
                    lines.append(f"  ... and {len(remaining) - 12} more")
            else:
                lines.append("  All fields set. Validate and commit.")
